ground_truth.title: Comparison of Social Media Platforms– What Works Best in 2025? - SEO Design Chicago
ground_truth.author: Nick Antonopoulos
ground_truth.main_content:
This article is your guide for a social media platform comparison between the eight most popular platforms. It will provide user data for each platform, along with two pros and two cons of using them. This social media platform comparison will be helpful for any business deciding which social media platforms will be most effective for them.

There is a wide variety of social media sites available, and there are differences between each platform. Different social media sites have different amounts of monthly active users and a different target audience. The right social media marketing platforms for your brand might be different than the best social media platforms for another type of business or industry.

Facebook is the most popular social media platform with more than 3.4 billion monthly active users as of early 2025. The targeting capabilities of Facebook and its Ads platform are some of the best that exist. However, in recent years, Facebook has become a platform that you need to pay to get the most out of, and conversion rates tend to be very low, hovering around 1%.

Instagram has over one billion monthly active users and is one of the best platforms for featuring photos or videos. Instagram's engagement rate is about 70% higher than Facebook's. However, captions on Instagram do not support clickable links, and since it relies on photos or short videos, you cannot explain your post much further.

Snapchat has much more of an appeal to younger users, with 69% of thirteen to seventeen year olds using it. Snapchat is good for creating a sense of urgency around your product, as nothing lasts longer than 24 hours on the app. However, unless your target market is tweens to college age kids, Snapchat is not the right platform for you.

Twitter's usage by American adults is lower than other networks, with only 22% of US adults using it. Twitter is a platform that allows you to connect with your audience on a personal level. However, Twitter is a very time sensitive platform, and for your posts to be the most effective, you need to tweet at the right time.

Pinterest has over 300 million monthly active users worldwide. Pinterest has a very high conversion rate for a social media site, with 50% of users saying they have purchased a product online after seeing it on Pinterest. However, Pinterest is most effective for visual content, and it is restricted to photos with no videos on Pinterest.

TikTok was downloaded 738 million times in 2019 alone and has surpassed 1.5 billion monthly active users in late 2024, making it the fastest-growing social platform. The most notable advantage of TikTok is that it allows you to get very creative. However, utilizing their advertisements can quickly get very expensive, and no one knows how long TikTok will last.

YouTube ranks higher than Facebook in usage by all US adults, with 73% of US adults using YouTube. Posting on YouTube can help your SEO rankings, as Google owns YouTube. However, YouTube is not going to promote your videos for you, and creating content for YouTube is much more time consuming than other platforms.

LinkedIn is most popular with people that have professional careers. The US has significantly more LinkedIn users than any other country in the world with 160 million users. A LinkedIn profile can improve SEO rankings for a small business. However, LinkedIn can quickly get expensive to access additional services, and most LinkedIn users will receive a lot of spam messages.

Social media marketing is an inexpensive tool that should be in every company's arsenal to reach more social media users.
